articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal! Eintracht Frankfurt 0, FC Bayern München 1. Kim Min-Jae (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Thomas Müller following a corner.

Goal! Eintracht Frankfurt 1, FC Bayern München 1. Omar Marmoush (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Ansgar Knauff with a through ball.

Goal! Eintracht Frankfurt 2, FC Bayern München 1. Hugo Ekitiké (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Omar Marmoush following a fast break.
Goal! Eintracht Frankfurt 2, FC Bayern München 2. Dayot Upamecano (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner following a corner.
Thomas Müller (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Mahmoud Dahoud replaces Ellyes Skhiri.
Goal! Eintracht Frankfurt 2, FC Bayern München 3. Michael Olise (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Junior Dina Ebimbe replaces Hugo Ekitiké.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Kingsley Coman replaces Serge Gnabry.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Mathys Tel replaces Harry Kane because of an injury.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Jean-Mattéo Bahoya replaces Ansgar Knauff.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Mario Götze replaces Hugo Larsson.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Can Uzun replaces Farès Chaïbi.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Konrad Laimer replaces Michael Olise.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Eric Dier replaces Dayot Upamecano because of an injury.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. João Palhinha replaces Aleksandar Pavlovic.

Goal! Eintracht Frankfurt 3, FC Bayern München 3. Omar Marmoush (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Junior Dina Ebimbe with a headed pass following a fast break.
Omar Marmoush (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card for excessive celebration.
Second Half ends, Eintracht Frankfurt 3, FC Bayern München 3.

article.headline: Last-gasp goal earns Frankfurt draw against leaders Bayern
article.description: Eintracht Frankfurt's Bundesliga top scorer Omar Marmoush scored twice, with his stoppage time equaliser snatching a 3-3 home draw against Bayern Munich.
article.linkText: Last-gasp goal earns Frankfurt draw vs. Bayern

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Omar Marmoush slots in the goal for Eintracht Frankfurt) 2024/10/06 ESHEET

article.story:
Eintracht Frankfurt's Bundesliga top scorer Omar Marmoush scored twice, with his stoppage time equaliser snatching a 3-3 home draw against Bundesliga leaders Bayern Munich on Sunday.

Marmoush, who had brought the sides level at 1-1 in the first half, completed a quick break in added time to bag the last-gasp goal that salvaged a point and made it three matches without a win in all competitions for the Bavarians.

Bayern now have 14 points and lead the standings on goal difference ahead of RB Leipzig, who were 1-0 winners at Heidenheim earlier on Sunday. Eintracht are third on 13.

Bayern coach Vincent Kompany will be relieved to go into the international break still top but must quickly figure out how his team got caught three times on the break, with Eintracht's late equaliser being their best chance in the second half.

The result also piles pressure on Kompany in his first season at Bayern following their slip-ups in Europe and the Bundesliga in the past week.

Bayern, desperate to bounce back from their midweek Champions League loss at Aston Villa and last week's Bundesliga draw at home to Bayer Leverkusen, went ahead when Kim Min-jae slotted in after a quarter of an hour following a strong start.

They should have had at least a couple of goals in that period but their joy was short lived, with Marmoush completing a lightning-quick break with a fine low shot past goalkeeper Manuel Neuer to level seven minutes later.

The Egypt forward, who has eight goals in six league games so far, then delivered a perfectly timed 35th minute assist in another counter-attack for Hugo Ekitike to score.

Bayern launched their own comeback three minutes later and levelled through Dayot Upamecano's shot from close range after a goalmouth scramble.

The visitors went back in front through Michael Olise's superb left-footed strike in the 53rd and should have scored again with substitute Kingsley Coman late in the game.

Marmoush, however, outsmarted the Bayern defence once more in stoppage time to race through and grab the equaliser, with Bayern's Thomas Mueller hitting the post with the last kick of the game.
